hero.title: Installation Guide
hero.subtitle: Professional results with DIY simplicity. Follow our step-by-step instructions for a perfect installation every time.

installMethodsSection.title: Choose Your Mount Type
installMethodsSection.subtitle: Select the installation method that works best for your windows and style preferences.

installMethods.title: Inside Mount
installMethods.description: Install within the window frame for a clean, built-in look. Best for windows with sufficient depth.
cons: May allow some light leakage on sides

installMethods.title: Outside Mount
installMethods.description: Install on the wall above the window frame. Provides maximum light blocking and makes windows appear larger.

toolsSection.title: Tools You'll Need
toolsSection.subtitle: Gather these tools before you begin. Most are common household items.

videoTutorialsSection.title: Video Tutorials
videoTutorialsSection.subtitle: Watch our detailed video guides for visual step-by-step instructions.

stepByStepSection.title: Step-by-Step Instructions
stepByStepSection.subtitle: Follow these {count} simple steps for a professional installation.

steps.title: Prepare Your Space
steps.content: Clear the area around your window. Lay down a drop cloth if needed. Have all your tools within reach. Remove any existing window treatments.
steps.tip: Take photos of your window before starting for reference.

steps.title: Mark Your Bracket Positions

contentByMethod.inside: Hold the bracket inside the window frame, about 2-3 inches from each side. Mark the screw holes with a pencil. Ensure brackets are level.
contentByMethod.outside: Measure and mark 2-3 inches above the window frame. Extend 3-4 inches beyond the window width on each side for optimal coverage. Use a level to ensure straight placement.

steps.tip: For wide windows, add a center bracket for extra support.

steps.title: Pre-Drill Holes
steps.content: Using a drill bit slightly smaller than your screws, pre-drill holes at your marked positions. If not drilling into a stud, insert drywall anchors.
steps.tip: Start with a smaller drill bit and work up to avoid splitting the wood.

steps.title: Mount the Brackets
steps.content: Attach the brackets using the provided screws. Ensure they are secure and level. Double-check alignment before fully tightening.
steps.tip: Do not overtighten - stop when the bracket is snug against the surface.

steps.title: Install the Shade/Curtain
steps.content: Carefully align the shade or curtain rod with the mounted brackets. For roller shades, insert the pin end first, then snap the control end into place.
steps.tip: Have a helper hold the shade steady while you secure it.

steps.title: Test and Adjust
steps.content: Operate your shade or curtain several times to ensure smooth movement. Check that it hangs level and straight. Make any necessary adjustments to the brackets.
steps.tip: If the shade binds, loosen the brackets slightly and reposition.

needHelpSection.title: Need Help?
needHelpSection.subtitle: Our installation experts are here to assist. Schedule a free video consultation or visit our help center for more resources.

proTip: Always double-check your measurements before drilling. It's easier to adjust a pencil mark than to fill unnecessary holes. If you're unsure, order our installation template for perfect bracket placement every time.
